title: Kanban View Across Multiple Boards (Support in dashboards)
excerpt: I would like to create a Kanban board with the pulses in multiple boards. It possible to make a table with multiple boards in Dashboards, but not a Kanban board.

posts.author: john.dasharion (John Dasharion)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to create a Kanban board with the pulses in multiple boards. It possible to make a table with multiple boards in Dashboards, but not a Kanban board.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @john.dasharion

Are you asking if you can have multiple board kanban widget in Dashboards? Not sure I understood?

posts.author: john.dasharion (John Dasharion)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! Exactly! Sorry I wasn’t more clear

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Right now, we don’t have a kanban widget in the dashboards but I’ll pass your feedback along! Can you describe your use case which will give me more weight when pushing for this feature?

posts.author: AlbertV (Albert)
posts.cookedHtml: Any estimation when we could have it? This is to me one of the most important things missing. That way you can check your portfolio or filter by your team so you can see the status of the tasks assigned to your team. It is essential for a project manager!

posts.author: sisrael1 (Sam Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com,

We too would like this type of feature. My thought was to have “Kanban Everything” tool, the way there is a Search Everything tool, but a dashboard widget might work as well.

Our use case is that we would like to able to see and re-prioritize what everyone is working on, across all boards, in daily stand ups and weekly meetings.

To get closer to this I’ve been doing the following:

  1. We have a standard status column that we use in all boards as a team requirement. Boards can have their own additional unique status columns if needed, but this provides a standard that can be seen across the entire instance.
  2. Every week I export each board to excel, and trim down to the key columns we need for kanban review, and aggregate them all into one spreadsheet.
  3. I import this new spreadsheet as a new board. I make the owner names into statuses, and group the items by owner.
  4. Add a kanban view to the board and use that in the meeting.

While it allows us to see and discuss all our current work, we can’t actually use the board to update anything, since these aren’t the true work item.

posts.author: KevinAtomic (Kevin Willingham)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was about to write a feature request for this as well but since this popped up as a previous post I figured I would add my thoughts here. We’ve hit a pain point where some PMs and directors really like the Kanban view to get a quick overview of project status and as possible day to day employee capacity. But of course we can’t pull that information in across all boards like we can with other views.

Would love to see this added as an option to the Dashboards either part of the table widget or as its own.

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with @sisrael1 that a Kanban Everything view would be great! You could add boards to it and then allow it to be filtered (by tag or other status column). We do “sprints” that cover items across multiple boards and it would be soooo helpful to be able to Kanban it all together for a single view

posts.author: Nemeo (Benoit Richert)
posts.cookedHtml:
We really miss this feature too! Same rationales:

  • For our sprint transitions, to move quickly sprint statuses while being cross-project
  • For ourselves, to update to status of our tasks from several board from new to working on it, the in review, then done…

posts.author: Phill (Phill Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: I too would love to have a place where our team members could see in Kanban format all the tasks they are assigned.

posts.author: Phill (Phill Allen)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a rough idea of what our team would like to have as a Kanban dashboard. We would be able to sort by status and people to get an overview from multiple boards. A master to-do list in Kanban format. Each Kanban item would also pull in the board name as reference (as shown on the bottom right corners, e.g. A079435 Nikon 2.0)

Kanban777×619 63 KB

posts.author: sisrael1 (Sam Israel)
posts.cookedHtml: This is exactly what we’ve been envisioning! Now that other members can see this idea, I hope it starts getting more votes. Thanks, Phil.

posts.author: AudiencePrecision (Sean Gerraty)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi - i would also like to upvote this functionality. Cheers, Sean

posts.author: Nick1 (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml:
100% need this!

A great example - One of our teams uses Kanban for general team tasks - but the team members like to be able to see their tasks AND add their own that they don’t want to clutter the main board with.

Currently they do this with Trello using the mirrored cards power up. Something gets assigned to them on the team Trello Kanban board, they mirror it to their own board where it sits in with their own tasks and they can switch to whichever view style they like.

Edit - I just ran through the system with one of our staff, and they said they would be happy to switch but would keep their Trello board to organise their own day / tasks and manually copy assigned tasks from Monday… which seems insane. They said there is a lot of personal tasks they usually add (like booking doctors appointments etc.), that they want reminding of during the work day as well as minor work tasks that don’t come from the team like sorting something with HR / IT, doing timesheets etc.

posts.author: kerstyn (Kerstyn)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news on whether this will be implemented? We use a kanban style approach to project management so the ‘My Week’ feature is pretty much useless for us. Being able to create personal kanban dashboards from a number of different boards would be fantastic.

posts.author: peterletten (Letten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes Plzzzzzzzzzzzz!
This is almost a deal breaker for us - since dates arent too fixed, its more about prioritising today, tomorrow, next week - as per the any.do functionality

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just a bump on this topic from my side.

Will a kanban dashboard widget be implemented at some point?

posts.author: Tanner (Tanner Dahlin)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know if there are other task management softwares that allow this feature?

posts.author: colebickham (Cole Bickham)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this. I just need to be able to get a simple Kanban snapshot of what my team is doing today and for the next few days for us to use in daily standups and to help them keep focused throughout the day. A Kanban dashboard board is the easiest way for them to view and easily adjust their immediate and upcoming tasks from all of our projects.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I am still looking for this

posts.author: srosato (Steven Rosato)
posts.cookedHtml:
I thought initially I could manage everything with just sprint board or other table views, but I agree that an Everytying Kanban is a must for someone who needs to work on multiple projects/clients. Right now I use the dashboard to agglomerate all my table views, but an Everything Kanban would indeed make this much more easy to be productive like a Toyota car factory

One first iteration could be to have Everything with filters. Another iteration could introduce Kanban boards within Kanban boards for an even broader view of what is happening in the entire business

posts.author: ClaudiaH (Claudia Hidalgo Montenegro)
posts.cookedHtml: Kanban is a basic tool that is present in every managment software, I can’t understand why something this basic hasn’t been developed yet. If I need a Kanban for my backlog (all the activities for all my projects) I have to create 1 board for all my projects, instead of 1 board for 1 project, wich turns a flexible tool like Monday to something rigid. I have to use 2 management software and if it dosn’t change I will have to look for another tool for my company. Please, listen to the community and look at your competition, this functionality is really needed!

posts.author: smvarieur (Shelby Varieur)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m trying to manage multiple projects at a time and love the dashboard option but the one MAJOR missing piece is the ability to create a kanban widget to consolidate the individual boards. Kanban is my favorite way to view assigned items but I’m unable to get the consolidated view I need, which makes it hard to prioritize tasks across projects when there is 1 owner. I don’t think I’m the first to request this but had a hard time finding similar posts. Thank you!!!

posts.author: jennifer_thecapacity (Jennifer Ruppelt)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d love to see a Kanban Everything dashboard to bring my cross-board “to-do” list together in one place.

posts.author: Kathy_K_TMT (Kathy)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com Will there be any update on this?

posts.author: Kathy_K_TMT (Kathy)
posts.cookedHtml: Will there be any update on having all boards selected > Dashboard > Kanban style (widget)?

posts.author: Kathy_K_TMT (Kathy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Continuing the discussion from Kanban View Across Multiple Boards:

WIll there be any update on having a Kanban style widget for Dashboard where we can select all of the boards we’d like displayed?

posts.author: croman653 (CR)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree! This is a critical functionality for those of us who prefer a Kanban view to a Table view. I can’t believe it’s not an option already; I know competing services offer this as a possibility.

posts.author: YaseminD (YaseminD)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree with this, the Kanban view is ideal for dashboards and sadly not available.

posts.author: J3SS (Jessica)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bringing this one back to light…
We also need this dashboard - has there been any progress since original entry? Any news?

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this ever coming? Just noticed now that the Kanban is not available for dashboards… sad!

posts.author: SteveWhite (Stephen Whitmarsh)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a basic need, especially since Monday doesn’t have any convenient organization between e.g. a master board and project boards. The functionality only seems to assume a limited number of boards, without any actualy integration that is practical, with no workload, time management, etc. over boards, or either so shallow or overly complicated with automation, that it doesn’t really work for any serieus project management beyond a single project.

posts.author: SteveWhite (Stephen Whitmarsh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same question as here: Kanban View Across Multiple Boards (Support in dashboards)
This is not the first obvious functionality that is missing. I really regret even trying Monday out at this point, and will see if I can get back to e.g. Wrike

posts.author: Xavier1 (Xavier Vilars)
posts.cookedHtml: Seems we are waiting this widget for 4 years now…

posts.author: Tooba (Tooba Ahmed)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a very useful feature @Kyliehuberman @shakedt can we get this added to the product list for next release

posts.author: JimONeill (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been trying to work out a way to create a client-facing dashboard or doc that we can add our clients to that will give them an overview of their project.

A kanban board on that dashboard or doc that I can sort either by task status or project phase (which would either be a group or an additional status column) would be required for this to be at all useful. It doesn’t even need to be client-editable, just a view!

Neither of these things is possible right now as far as I can tell. Seems like there’s a lot of community support for upgrading Kanban features - but this is still open 3.5 years later

posts.author: cfreemansa (Claudie Freeman)
posts.cookedHtml:
A single view of all tasks across all projects is required for daily standups and resource management.

There is a Kanban view per “Full Product Development” template. But one cannot view a consolidated kanban board of all tasks for multiple kanban boards. I therefore cannot display a single Kanban board in daily standups for different teams working on different projects where tasks are created in multiple “Full Product Development” templates.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this.
When can we expect an update?

posts.author: ultrasonicjamie (James McIntosh)
posts.cookedHtml: Please, we’re going down the route of re-creating mondays tasks on a giant white board, just to build a KanBan board, it woudl eb so usefull if this was a Dashboard widget so I can bring all our projects to the same place, given that dashboard linked boards is a licence item, we’d even consider an enterprise licence if we hit the limits, it woudl be so usefull, all teh data is there, we jsut need to see it in the right format.

posts.author: KBat (Karen Battist)
posts.cookedHtml: This is definitely needed. I also have requested this feature. Following the status of this and hope that it is implemented very soon!

posts.author: Julia1 (Julia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Phil,
Thank you for visualizing your idea.
Would the columns of your kanban board (On Hold, Next, To DO, Done) match with a status field values you have in your boards ? Would you expect that the status of a task changes when you move it from one Kanban column to the other?
As you suggested to sort tasks by statuses, I guess you have the same status field and values in all your boards?

Thank you

posts.author: LaurenY (Lauren Yammine)
posts.cookedHtml: Our development team wants to be able to view their work across projects in a Kanban view for capacity planning and to implement a more agile process. We have been trying using the ‘My Work’ section, but it also is lacking the Kanban view. Lists don’t work for everyone - and we are trying to create a more inclusive workspace for folks.

posts.author: kranthi_thoughtflow (Kranthi Kiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,

Excited to share that our team at ThoughtFlow is working on an app to show Items across multiple boards on a common kanban board. Its a dashboard widget that works when you have a similar status column ( similar names and states ) across multiple boards.

Here’s a short demo of the app

posts.author: troach70 (Troy Roach)
posts.cookedHtml:
I did lots of looking around for another app that can show Kanbans within Kanbans (or board views) and it seems clickup.com has this feature included in the free account. Not only can you see a Board View at the individual Workspace Dashboard (Overview) level, but you can also see a Board View of ALL your workspaces.

Hope this helps.

posts.author: AnnHolt (Ann Holt)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely something that I’m looking for. I work across multiple projects and I am trying to use My Work to see all my work, but would prefer to visualize it using Kanban. Failing that just having a way of combining multiple boards into a Kanban would work for me.

posts.author: James.Deguara (James Deguara)
posts.cookedHtml: Something like this is definitely needed. Even to just be able to display the kanban view of a board in a dashboard is not possible! Are Monday doing anything about this?

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, you can achieve this outcome by using “Same Item Multiple Board” app. You can add all items into one board and set “kanban” view.

posts.author: James.Deguara (James Deguara)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @garfield-labs I understand that this is a paid app. I was hoping that Monday would extend their support allow us to use all common views within their dashboard

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my vote for a Kanban Widget in Dashboards (within monday itself). I am currently rolling out CRM with an original deals board and a change orders board. The Commerical team is setting priorities for both and it would be ideal to be able to pull both boards into a Kanban view to display the two sets of high priority items together.

posts.author: Stela1 (Stela)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great news, monday.com community!

We’ve heard your requests for a multiple board Kanban view, and we’re thrilled to announce the launch of our Kanbanify app in the monday.com marketplace!

Kanbanify streamlines your workflow by connecting multiple boards to one powerful Kanban view where you can add filters to prioritize your/your teams work and stay focused.

There is also a free plan to get you started!

This is just the beginning! We’re constantly adding new features based on your feedback. Let us know what you think and suggest features you’d love to see!

Thank you,

Stela (Head of marketing)

Appstorm

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Heather, it’s possible to achieve with the app Same Item Multiple Boards - You can bring items from 2 boards into one and set a kanban view

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @garfield-labs thanks for the tip! I will keep this app in mind for future use cases, looks as though it could be really useful. For this particular use case I really don’t want to have to maintain an additional, master board, solely to support Kanban reporting. So will go without at this time, hoping that monday will support directly.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @Stela1 thanks for letting us know, this is a very interesting option for this particular use case at our org. I will check it out.

posts.author: Stela1 (Stela)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

We’re thrilled to announce the launch of our Kanbanify app in the monday.com marketplace!

Kanbanify streamlines your workflow by connecting multiple boards to one powerful Kanban view where you can add filters to prioritize your/your teams work and stay focused. You can also combine subitems as separate cards in one Kanban view.

There is also a free plan to get you started!

This is just the beginning! We’re constantly adding new features based on your feedback. Let us know what you think and suggest features you’d love to see!

Thank you,

Stela (Head of marketing)

Appstorm

posts.author: kranthi_thoughtflow (Kranthi Kiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey folks,

Sharing a few updates on the Multi Board Kanban app that our team has been working on for the last few months. With this app you can generate a Kanban from multiple boards using similar status columns.

Here are some of the features

Drag and drop items from Multiple Boards from one place

Split vertically into swim lanes with another status column
Make a two dimensional view with status and priority across multiple boards

image985×782 41.6 KB

Add items into any connected board along with a status
Useful for delegating work into multiple boards

See sub items as cards
useful for teams using sub items as tasks

Abides to the filter in the dashboard header

Filter items assigned to a team member
See what a specific user is working on

Customize your cards to see what’s important

Reorder lanes to suit your workflow

Here’s what some of our users are saying

image325×679 93.4 KB

I invite you to try it out from the marketplace and share your feedback. Thank you

posts.author: xmasterg (TZ)
posts.cookedHtml: How about now? 5 years later…

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: I am still looking for this feature also. I realize there is an app solution, but would really like to see this native in monday, which seems very natural given all of the other features in Dashboards.

posts.author: Leah1 (Leah)
posts.cookedHtml:
To add to this request title Kanban View Across Multiple Boards (but not just for dashboards), it should be a feature in the Portfolio board too!
The portfolio board does have a Kanban board view but it only lets you select the Portfolio Board phases. I want to sort the kanban by my project boards (attached to the portfolio board) phase. It is a Portfolio Board so it should be a Kanban with the multiple projects and a choice to sort by the phase of the project boards in the portfolio.
Thank you.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature should be more and more feasible now that there is a system to group statuses even if they are not exactly the same. (This system is used in dashboard filters when choosing to filter “All boards”.)
When can we expect this natively without additional costs for third party apps?

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team Thank you so much for your feedback around the need for Kanban view across multiple boards, we hear you!

At this time, this feature isn’t on our long-term roadmap, as we’re currently focused on other high-impact priorities that are further along in development. That said, we know this is an important feature for many of you, so we’ll continue to evaluate it as we plan ahead.

We’re always listening, and we’ll make sure to share any updates here when anything changes!
